description: Defines all available algorithms, their config structure, parameter specs, defaults, and constraints. The algorithm block is always required in every config file.

dmrg.description: Density Matrix Renormalization Group. Variationally optimizes an MPS toward the ground state of H by repeatedly sweeping left-right and solving a local two-site eigenvalue problem at each bond using the Lanczos algorithm.

options.local_dim: <int — auto-derived from system S, do not set manually>

type.description: Solver type identifier. Always 'lanczos' for DMRG.

krylov_dim.description: Krylov subspace dimension for the Lanczos eigensolver. Larger values improve convergence per iteration at higher cost per step. Default matches LanczosSolver constructor default in Algorithms_solvers.jl.
krylov_dim.note: GUI default is 20. Solver default is 4. Increase for hard-to-converge problems.

max_iter.description: Maximum Lanczos restart iterations per local site solve. More iterations = better local convergence at higher cost.
max_iter.note: GUI default is 10. Solver default is 100.

chi_max.description: Maximum MPS bond dimension (chi). Controls the entanglement that can be represented. DMRG truncates SVD to this dimension after each two-site update. Larger chi = more accurate but slower and more memory.
chi_max.note: Start at 64 for quick runs. Converge by doubling: 64 → 128 → 256. chi_max^3 * N scales the cost.

cutoff.description: SVD truncation cutoff. Singular values below this threshold are discarded during MPS truncation. Acts as a second truncation criterion alongside chi_max — whichever is more restrictive wins.
cutoff.note: 1e-10 is standard. Decrease for higher precision. Increase to aggressively cap chi growth.

local_dim.description: Local Hilbert space dimension per site. AUTO-DERIVED from system S: d = 2S+1. For spinboson: this is the spin local dim only — the boson site uses nmax+1 internally. Do NOT set manually in the GUI; the config builder computes it.

n_sweeps.description: Total number of DMRG sweeps. Each sweep is one full left-right pass over all bonds. Convergence is typically reached in 10-50 sweeps depending on system size, chi_max, and gap.
n_sweeps.note: 20 sweeps is a good starting point. Monitor energy convergence — stop when dE/sweep < 1e-8.

output.description: DMRG saves MPS snapshots and energy values at each sweep.

convergence_guide.energy_convergence: Monitor dE between sweeps. Converged when |E(n) - E(n-1)| < 1e-8.
convergence_guide.chi_convergence: Converge by doubling chi_max while keeping n_sweeps fixed. Converged when observables don't change.
convergence_guide.typical_workflow: Start chi_max=64, n_sweeps=20. If energy still changing, double chi_max. Repeat.

tdvp.description: Time-Dependent Variational Principle. Evolves an MPS in real or imaginary time by projecting the time-dependent Schrödinger equation onto the MPS tangent space. Uses Krylov subspace matrix exponential for local site time steps. Two-site variant with SVD truncation at each step.

options.local_dim: <int — auto-derived from system S, do not set manually>

type.description: Solver type identifier. Always 'krylov_exponential' for TDVP.

krylov_dim.description: Krylov subspace dimension for the matrix exponential solver. Larger values improve accuracy of the local time step at higher per-step cost. Convergence checked against tol.
krylov_dim.note: GUI default is 30. Solver default is 30. Increase if tol is not being met.

tol.description: Convergence tolerance for the Krylov exponential. The solver checks |output(p) - output(p-1)| elementwise and stops when all components are within tol. Tighter tol = more accurate local step but more Krylov vectors needed.
tol.note: GUI default is 1e-8. Solver default is 1e-12.

evol_type.description: Type of time evolution. 'real' evolves with e^{-iHt} (physical real-time dynamics). 'imaginary' evolves with e^{-Ht} (imaginary time, converges to ground state like a soft DMRG).

allowed_values.real: Real time evolution: e^{-iHdt}. Unitary, norm-preserving. Use for quantum dynamics, quench problems, time correlation functions.
allowed_values.imaginary: Imaginary time evolution: e^{-Hdt}. Non-unitary, converges toward ground state. Alternative to DMRG for ground state search.

dt.description: Time step per sweep. Each sweep advances the state by dt. Total simulated time = dt * n_sweeps. Smaller dt = more accurate but more sweeps needed to reach same total time.
dt.note: dt=0.05 with n_sweeps=100 gives total time T=5.0. Decrease dt if observables show oscillation artifacts.

chi_max.description: Maximum MPS bond dimension during time evolution. Entanglement grows as O(t) under generic dynamics, so chi_max must be large enough to capture the entanglement at the final time. Truncation introduces controllable errors.
chi_max.note: For short times chi_max=64 is often sufficient. For long times or highly entangled states, increase to 128 or 256.

cutoff.description: SVD truncation cutoff for MPS compression after each time step. Works alongside chi_max.

local_dim.description: Local Hilbert space dimension per site. AUTO-DERIVED from system S: d = 2S+1. Do NOT set manually.

n_sweeps.description: Total number of time steps (sweeps). Each sweep advances the state by dt. Total simulated time = dt * n_sweeps.
n_sweeps.note: With dt=0.05, n_sweeps=100 gives T=5.0. With dt=0.01, n_sweeps=500 gives T=5.0.

output.description: TDVP saves MPS snapshots and time values at each sweep.

accuracy_guide.time_step_error: O(dt^2) per step from Trotter-like splitting. Halve dt and check if observables change.
accuracy_guide.truncation_error: Controlled by chi_max and cutoff. Monitor truncation error per step printed by the runner.
accuracy_guide.total_error: Accumulates as n_sweeps * per_step_error. Longer simulations need smaller dt and larger chi_max.

ed_spectrum.description: Full or partial exact diagonalization of the Hamiltonian matrix. Computes eigenvalues and eigenvectors directly. No initial state needed. Best for small systems where exact results are required.

n_states.description: Number of lowest eigenstates to compute. If omitted or null, computes the full spectrum (all D eigenstates). If set, computes only the lowest n_states eigenstates.
n_states.note: Leave empty for full diagonalization. Set to 1 for ground state only. use_sparse=true requires n_states to be set.

use_sparse.description: Whether to use sparse (Lanczos-based) diagonalization instead of dense LAPACK. Sparse is faster for large D when only a few eigenstates are needed. Dense is more reliable and gives all states.

allowed_values.false: Dense diagonalization via LAPACK eigen(). Computes all D eigenstates. Reliable for any system size within memory limits.
allowed_values.true: Sparse Lanczos diagonalization. Only valid when n_states is set. Faster for large D with small n_states. Recommended for N > 12.

use_sparse.constraint: use_sparse=true requires n_states to be explicitly set.

output.description: ED Spectrum saves all computed eigenvalues and eigenvectors to results.jld2.

spin_half.N_le_12: Fast, practical. Full spectrum in seconds.
spin_half.N_eq_14: Near memory limit (~4 GB). Use sparse + n_states for ground state.
spin_half.N_ge_16: Impractical for ED. Use DMRG instead.

spinboson.guidance: D = (nmax+1) * d^N_spins. With nmax=4, S=0.5: N_spins <= 10 is practical.

ed_time_evolution.description: Exact time evolution via eigendecomposition. First diagonalizes H to get all eigenstates, then evolves the initial state as |ψ(t)⟩ = Σₙ cₙ e^{-iEₙt} |n⟩. Exact within machine precision. Requires initial state.

dt.description: Time step between saved snapshots. The state is evolved by dt at each step using the exact eigendecomposition. Unlike TDVP, there is no discretization error — dt only controls output resolution, not accuracy.
dt.note: Smaller dt = more time resolution in saved data. Does not affect accuracy since evolution is exact.

n_steps.description: Number of time steps to simulate. Total simulated time = dt * n_steps.

output.description: ED time evolution saves the state vector and time at each step.

comparison_with_tdvp.accuracy: ED time evolution is numerically exact (machine precision). TDVP has O(dt^2) Trotter error and MPS truncation error.
comparison_with_tdvp.system_size: ED is limited to small systems (N <= 14 for spin-1/2). TDVP scales to N = 1000+.
comparison_with_tdvp.use_case: Use ED for benchmarking TDVP on small systems, or for exact results where system size permits.

algorithm_selection_guide.description: How to choose the right algorithm for your problem.

rules.ground_state_large_system: Use dmrg. Works up to N=1000. Most accurate for gapped 1D systems.
rules.ground_state_small_system: Use ed_spectrum with use_sparse=false for full spectrum, or n_states=1 for ground state only.
rules.time_dynamics_large_system: Use tdvp with evol_type=real. Accuracy limited by chi_max and dt.
rules.time_dynamics_small_system: Use ed_time_evolution for exact dynamics. Benchmark tdvp against this.
rules.imaginary_time_ground_state: Use tdvp with evol_type=imaginary. Alternative to dmrg for hard convergence cases.
rules.excited_states: Use ed_spectrum with n_states=k for the k lowest states.
rules.benchmark_tdvp: Run ed_time_evolution on a small system, then run tdvp with same parameters. Compare observables.

time_evolution.description: Propagate an initial state in real time

imaginary_time.description: Ground state search via imaginary time propagation

local_dim_note.description: The local_dim field in DMRG and TDVP options is auto-derived by the GUI from system.S using d = floor(2*S + 1). It must be present in the config for the Julia runner to construct DMRGOptions/TDVPOptions correctly, but it should never be set manually — the config builder computes it automatically.
local_dim_note.spinboson_note: For spinboson systems, local_dim reflects the spin site dimension only. The boson site dimension (nmax+1) is handled separately inside the MPO/MPS builder and does not appear in DMRGOptions or TDVPOptions.
